title: helianthus strumosus
intro: Helianthus strumosus, also known as the prairie sunflower, is a perennial plant native to the prairies of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions and salves, which can provide health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from pain and itching. Additionally, the plant's roots and seeds have been used in herbal medicine to treat digestive issues and promote relaxation.
